title: TreeLaplacianWords
summary: Defines rank-general left-comb indices and evaluates the associated nested multilinear Laplacian word.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines rank-general left-comb indices and evaluates the associated nested multilinear Laplacian word.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/TreeLaplacianWords.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Number of vertices in a full binary tree with r ordered leaves. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, its defining expression is 2 times r minus 1.

A left-comb tree Laplacian index of total degree k. Its mathematical data are: power: Fin left Comb Vertex Count r maps to the natural numbers; sum eq: Finset.univ.sum fun v : Fin left Comb Vertex Count r maps to power v equals k.

Rank-general tree index alias. Later this can be widened from left-combs to arbitrary rooted binary trees without changing downstream recurrence code too much. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Comb Index.

Total Laplacian degree of a comb index. In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, and i of type comb index r k, its defining expression is Finset.univ.sum fun v : Fin left Comb Vertex Count r maps to I.power v.
correspondence.leanType: def totalPower {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) : ℕ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r, k in the natural numbers, and I of type Comb Index r k, I.total Power equals k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalPower_eq {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) : I.totalPower = k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Read a vertex power using a natural-number code, returning 0 outside the vertex range. This is convenient for recursive word evaluation.
correspondence.leanType: def powerAt {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) (i : ℕ) : ℕ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r, k in the natural numbers, I of type Comb Index r k, and v of type Fin left Comb Vertex Count r, I.power At v.val equals I.power v.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,k\colon \mathbb{N},\;I\colon \operatorname{CombIndex} r k,\;v\colon \operatorname{Fin} (\operatorname{leftCombVertexCount} r),\quad I.\operatorname{powerAt} v.\operatorname{val} = I.\operatorname{power} v
correspondence.leanType: theorem powerAt_of_fin {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : I.powerAt v.val = I.power v

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Add one unit of Laplacian power at a chosen vertex. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, i of type comb index r s, and v of type fin left comb vertex count r, its defining expression is power : equals Function.update I.power v I.power v plus 1 sum eq : equals by classical rw Finset.sum update of mem s : equals Finset.univ i : equals v Finset.mem univ v I.power I.power v plus 1 have hsum : I.power v plus ∑ x belongs to Finset.univ \ v, I.power x equals s : equals by rw ← Finset.sum eq add sum diff singleton s : equals Finset.univ i : equals v Finset.mem univ v I.power exact I.sum eq omega.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{addAt}(I,v)=\operatorname{power} = \operatorname{Function}.\operatorname{update} I.\operatorname{power} v (I.\operatorname{power} v + 1) \operatorname{sum\_eq} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{classical} \operatorname{rw} [\operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{sum\_update\_of\_mem} (s = \operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{univ}) (i = v) (\operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{mem\_univ} v) I.\operatorname{power} (I.\operatorname{power} v + 1)] \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hsum} \colon I.\operatorname{power} v + \sum x \in \operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{univ} \ {v}, I.\operatorname{power} x = s = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{rw} [← \operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{sum\_eq\_add\_sum\_diff\_singleton} (s = \operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{univ}) (i = v) (\operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{mem\_univ} v) I.\operatorname{power}] \operatorname{exact} I.\operatorname{sum\_eq} \operatorname{omega}
correspondence.leanType: def addAt {r s : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r s) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : CombIndex r (s + 1)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r, s in the natural numbers, I of type Comb Index r s, and v of type Fin left Comb Vertex Count r, I.add At v.power v equals I.power v plus 1.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,s\colon \mathbb{N},\;I\colon \operatorname{CombIndex} r s,\;v\colon \operatorname{Fin} (\operatorname{leftCombVertexCount} r),\quad (I.\operatorname{addAt} v).\operatorname{power} v = I.\operatorname{power} v + 1
correspondence.leanType: theorem addAt_power_same {r s : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r s) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : (I.addAt v).power v = I.power v + 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r, s in the natural numbers, I of type Comb Index r s, v, x of type Fin left Comb Vertex Count r, and hxv of type x is not equal to v, I.add At v.power x equals I.power x.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,s\colon \mathbb{N},\;I\colon \operatorname{CombIndex} r s,\;v,x\colon \operatorname{Fin} (\operatorname{leftCombVertexCount} r),\;hxv\colon x \ne v,\quad (I.\operatorname{addAt} v).\operatorname{power} x = I.\operatorname{power} x
correspondence.leanType: theorem addAt_power_ne {r s : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r s) {v x : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)} (hxv : x ≠ v) : (I.addAt v).power x = I.power x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Multinomial coefficient attached to a tree index. In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, and i of type comb index r k, its defining expression is Nat.factorial k : ℚ divided by Finset.univ.prod fun v : Fin left Comb Vertex Count r maps to Nat.factorial I.power v : ℚ.
correspondence.leanType: def multinomialCoeffQ {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) : ℚ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluate a left-comb word using a raw natural-number power function. The recursive convention is: r = 1: Delta^(p 0) u_0; r + 1: apply Delta^(p 0) to the product of the left r-comb using shifted powers p(i+1) and the final leaf Delta^(p(2r)) u_r. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, its defining expression is | 0, p, F maps to zero Conf | 1, p, F maps to Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian p 0 F 0 | r plus 2, p, F maps to let left Input : Cal Conf.Multi Input r plus 1 : equals the function sending i to F ⟨i.val, by have hi : equals i.is Lt omega⟩ let left Word : Function Conf : equals left Comb Eval From Cal Conf r plus 1 the function sending i to p i plus 1 left Input let last Leaf : Function Conf : equals Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian p 2 times r plus 2 minus 2 F ⟨r plus 1, by omega⟩ Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian p 0 the function sending U to left Word U times last Leaf U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{leftCombEvalFrom}(0, _p, _F)&=\operatorname{zero} \operatorname{Conf}\\\operatorname{leftCombEvalFrom}(1, p, F)&=\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} (p 0) (F 0)\\\operatorname{leftCombEvalFrom}(r + 2, p, F)&=\operatorname{let} \operatorname{leftInput} \colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} (r + 1) = i \longmapsto F ⟨i.\operatorname{val}, \operatorname{by} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hi} = i.\operatorname{isLt} \operatorname{omega}⟩ \operatorname{let} \operatorname{leftWord} \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{leftCombEvalFrom} \operatorname{CalConf} (r + 1) (i \longmapsto p (i + 1)) \operatorname{leftInput} \operatorname{let} \operatorname{lastLeaf} \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} (p (2 \cdot (r + 2) - 2)) (F ⟨r + 1, \operatorname{by} \operatorname{omega}⟩) \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} (p 0) (U \longmapsto \operatorname{leftWord} U \cdot \operatorname{lastLeaf} U)\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: def leftCombEvalFrom (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : (r : ℕ) → (ℕ → ℕ) → CalConf.MultiInput r → Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The rank-r multi-operator represented by a left-comb tree index. In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, i of type comb index r k, and calconf of type calculus conf, its defining expression is left Comb Eval From Cal Conf r I.power At.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r, k in the natural numbers, I of type Comb Index r k, and F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, I.to Operator Cal Conf F equals left Comb Eval From Cal Conf r I.power At F.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,k\colon \mathbb{N},\;I\colon \operatorname{CombIndex} r k,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\quad I.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} F = \operatorname{leftCombEvalFrom} \operatorname{CalConf} r I.\operatorname{powerAt} F
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : I.toOperator CalConf F = leftCombEvalFrom CalConf r I.powerAt F

references.locator: Main ambient construction and tangentiality results
references.note: Motivation for rank-general nested Laplacian words; the Lean tree complex goes beyond what is currently instantiated.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
